  fix(memory-core): implement retry-on-unload for openAiCompatible embeddings

---
Closes #11393

This PR implements an exponential backoff + fallback loop in the Memory Core MCP server for the `openAiCompatible` embedding provider to handle JIT model unload errors gracefully.

### Changes
- Updated `ai/services/memory-core/TextEmbeddingService.mjs` to detect HTTP 400 responses containing "Model was unloaded" and trigger a retry loop.
- Added `unloadRetryCount` (default: 3) and `unloadRetryDelayMs` (default: 500ms) to `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.mjs`.
- Added `NEO_OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_UNLOAD_RETRY_COUNT` and `NEO_OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_UNLOAD_RETRY_DELAY_MS` env bindings.
- Created `TextEmbeddingService.retry.spec.mjs` to test all retry semantics.

No regressions introduced. Test suite passes successfully.

### 🔬 Depth Floor

**Challenge OR documented search (per guide §7.1):**

**Challenge** — the detection scope is narrower than the empirically-observed substrate-friction class:

Current implementation at `TextEmbeddingService.mjs:115-119`:

```javascript
if (retriesLeft > 0 && err.message.includes('HTTP 400') && err.message.includes('Model was unloaded')) {
    // ... retry
}
```

This catches the literal "Model was unloaded while the request was still in queue" error returned by LM Studio when a previously-loaded model has been JIT-unloaded due to idle timeout. **Empirically verified in this session at 03:04Z** — that's the failure shape #11393's AC1 was written against.

**However:** in the same session at 03:17Z (~13 minutes later), I hit a DIFFERENT LM Studio error shape for what is structurally the same substrate-friction (model not resident, failed to JIT-warm-on-demand):

```
openAiCompatible embedding error HTTP 400:
{"error":"Failed to load model \"text-embedding-qwen3-embedding-8b\". Error: Operation canceled."}
```

This is the SAME substrate-friction-class as the original "Model was unloaded" — both indicate the embedding provider cannot serve the request and a retry-with-warmup-delay is the correct semantic response. The current detection does NOT catch this second shape and would propagate the error to the caller, breaking the AGENTS.md §0 Invariant 5 reliability gate that #11393 aimed to restore.

**This is genuinely on me as the ticket author** — I had the empirical evidence of the second-shape in `MESSAGE:36b84262` (filed at 03:17Z) but never propagated it back into #11393's AC1 text. You implemented per the literal AC correctly. Moving the goalposts mid-Cycle-1 would be the ticket-author anti-pattern; surfacing this as a follow-up ticket is the substrate-discipline-correct response.

**Rhetorical-Drift Audit (per guide §7.4):**

Mark N/A — PR body is concise + accurate; no substantive architectural prose to overshoot. The PR title and commit message match the literal AC scope without overclaiming.

**Findings:** Pass on Rhetorical-Drift dimension. Challenge documents the narrower-than-class detection scope as a separate-ticket-shape follow-up.
